chapter.title: Sermons
chapter.note: Peter Damian, sermon 47; Bernard of Clairvaux, sermon 62; Peter Damian, sermon 9, 48, and sermon on John

chapter.title: Gospels
chapter.incipit: Nomen libri evangelium grece bonum nuncium
chapter.note: Fols. 3r-4v: preface to Matthew; fols. 5r-5v: glossed prologue to Matthew; fols. 5v-79r: glossed Matthew; fols. 82r-82v: glossed prologue to Mark; fols. 83r-135r: glossed Mark; fol. 125v: preface to Luke; fols. 126r-204v: incomplete text of Luke (it begins with Luke 1:9); fols. 210r-256r: glossed John

chapter.title: Canon law formularies
chapter.incipit: H. dei gratia abbas illius loci B. clerico salutem
chapter.note: Law formularies; references to Oxford and its churches (St. Frideswide, St. Peter, Mary, Oseney, and Thame); dates 1193, 1194, 1199, and 1203; Kings Richard and John as well as Pope Innocent III are mentioned

Between the Gospels of Matthew and Mark, there are five folios with law formularies, which contain models for letters, sentences, donations, etc.; formularies are integral to the quire containing the end of the text of Matthew

chapter.title: Sermons for the liturgy
chapter.incipit: Hodie fratres karissimi redemptor noster ad nos venit
chapter.note: Sermons for Christmas day, Easter, Pentecost, and the beginning of Lent
